samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module. Detects rising edges of data_in and outputs a pulse data_out.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE">State during pulse detection.</state>
        <state id="END_PULSE">End of pulse state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="state">Current state of the pulse detection process.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="state">Next state of the pulse detection process.</reg>
        
        <always @(*) begin
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1 && ~data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = PULSE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                PULSE: begin
                    if(~data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = END_PULSE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE;
                    end
                end
                END_PULSE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = END_PULSE;
                    end
                end
                default: begin
                    next_state = INITIAL;
                end
            endcase
        end
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
                
                if(next_state == END_PULSE) begin
                    data_out <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    data_out <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Implements the pulse detection logic described above.</implementation>
</module>
